examples.title: Basic Compilation with g++
examples.description: Demonstrates how to compile and execute a simple C++ program using the g++ compiler
examples.explanation:
1. Create a C++ source file with .cpp extension
2. Use g++ compiler to compile: g++ source.cpp -o output
3. The -o flag specifies the output executable name
4. Run the compiled executable with ./programname

examples.title: Compilation with Multiple Source Files
examples.description: Shows how to compile a program with multiple source files and header files
examples.explanation:
1. Separate declaration (.h) and implementation (.cpp) files
2. Compile all source files together: g++ file1.cpp file2.cpp -o program
3. The linker combines object files into final executable
4. -I. flag tells compiler to look in current directory for headers

examples.title: Compilation Stages and Flags
examples.description: Demonstrates the four stages of C++ compilation with preprocessing, compilation, assembly, and linking
examples.explanation:
C++ compilation has 4 stages:
1. Preprocessing: Handles #include, #define directives
2. Compilation: Converts source to assembly code
3. Assembly: Creates object (.o) binary files
4. Linking: Combines object files with libraries to create executable

Common flags:
-g: Debug symbols
-O0/-O1/-O2/-O3: Optimization levels
-Werror: Treat warnings as errors
-std=c++11/14/17/20: C++ standard version
